evidence.q1: Section 16 refers to claims under Section 13 or Section 14, and all sections are numbered sequentially 1–18.
evidence.q2: Customer Data defined in Section 2 and used consistently, e.g., in Section 5 'to provide and improve the services, develop analytics, and perform other business purposes reasonably related to the service relationship.'
evidence.q3: No vague time/quantity phrasing; examples include 'within 72 hours' (Sec.6), 'forty-five days after Customer receives the invoice' (Sec.9), 'two years' (Sec.10).
evidence.q4: No archaic legal diction; uses modern phrases like 'entered into by and between,' 'as of the effective date,' and 'Vendor will.'
evidence.q5: No internally contradictory or grammatically broken sentences; a typical well‑formed sentence: 'Customer will pay fees for services performed before the effective termination date plus undisputed reimbursable expenses approved in writing and incurred before that date.'
evidence.q6: Register is consistent throughout; e.g., operative verbs ('Vendor will,' 'Customer will') and formal tone are uniform across all sections.
